prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr, ye be askin' why the sky be shinin' blue as me ol' piece o' eight? Well, ye see, 'tis the mighty sun what's shinin' bright an' the earth's atmosphere what's doin' the trick. The sun's rays, they be shootin' every which way, scatterin' those colors far and wide. 

But here's the rub - the blue an' violet, they get scattered more than the rest, ye see. It's called Rayleigh scatterin', named after some bloke who figgered it all out. The wee droplets o' water an' dust in the air, they be reflectin' the blue right back at ye. 

So when ye look up to the heavens an' see that grand expanse o' blue, ye can thank ol' Sol up yonder what's makin' all that pretty sky shine. Now, how 'bout ye help me swab the deck before that blasted moon rises an' we have to set the sails at midnight? I heard tell the Spaniards be sailin' these waters, keepin' an eye out fer plunder!
